Product Launch Formula PLC 1
I want to you about the very survival of your business – or if you haven’t started a
business yet, the survival of your future business. If you have an online business, or
you’re using the internet or social media to drive leads and sales into your offline
business, you have to listen because the rules have changed in the last 12 months.
I know that might sound overly dramatic, but stick with me for a little bit. I’ve got a
long track record and I don’t tend towards drama, and frankly, I think I’ve earned this
one. I’m Jeff Walker and I’ve spent the last eight years teaching entrepreneurs how to
launch their businesses and how to launch their products. I’ve got thousands of
thousands of students who have done literally hundreds of millions of dollars in
launches.
When I first started out, I really had to try to convince people that they needed to do a
launch. Instead of just releasing your product and hoping it did well, you had to put
together a structured, well engineered product launch – a structured and well
engineered business launch. But now after eight years my students’ success speaks for
itself, you see launches all over the place.
My students are doing it in every kind of business, every kind of market you can
imagine, with every kind of product you can imagine, from dog training to massage
therapy, to mixed martial arts, to raw food, to health food, to learning guitar. Yes, even
horse ballet. They’ve used these business launches and product launches to become
known as leaders, to get expert positioning in their market, to become the guru, so to
speak. And they’ve used it to build real businesses. Many of them have started small
and progressively built their launch after launch and built their positioning, built their
momentum, and built their expert status.
In fact, I’ll bet there’s proof right now in your inbox. No matter what market you’re
interested in, what market you follow, even look at a market that you might have an
interest in, say for me it’s learning guitar. I subscribe to all these people who teach
about how to learn guitar. If you just go to your inbox, whatever you’re interested in,
whatever you’re subscribed to, go take a look and you will see the leaders in that
market, the experts in that market, the people that are positioned as the go-to people
in that market, go take a look and I guarantee you that you’re going to see them doing
launches, especially if you’ve gotten emails from them for the last 6 to 12 months. You’ll
see them putting together these product launches, because that’s what they do. That’s
what experts do, that’s how experts become known as experts. That’s how gurus get
that guru positioning is they do launches.
In fact, I bet you’re probably subscribed to different people that are teaching about
business. I know I’m getting a whole bunch of stuff about business. There’s no end to
the products out there about business, about marketing, about entrepreneurship. Take
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module5v1.0

-- 2 of 12 --

a look at what they’re doing. Not what they’re teaching, but what they’re actually doing.
I guarantee you they are doing product launches, because this is how you get expert
positioning in your market.
There’s even some experts, gurus, or teachers out there that tell you, “Don’t do
launches, do something else. Do this. Do that. This works. This is the best thing.” But
don’t look at what they’re saying, go take a look at what they’re actually doing. Go look
at their emails and if you can track back over six to 12 months, you will see they are
doing these launches. That’s because they work and they keep on working.
In a minute I’m going to share with you the one core strategy that really fuels these
launches, that drives the great launches. But, first, I need to warn you. There is one
problem. There are some dark clouds on the horizon. I’m seeing a lot of broken
launches.
I just look at my inbox and I’m thinking, “You didn’t really do that, did you? Oh no.
Oops.” Then I hear back on the results and they’re not going anywhere. That’s a real
problem because a broken launch can really set you back. It’s not just a onetime oops,
it can really set you back months or even years.
Those problems are all easy to avoid, and I’ll get to that in a minute. The thing is it’s
not all bad news. In fact, it’s mostly good news. It’s mostly great news, because I’m
seeing these great launches, I keep hearing back about these great launches from my
students and clients, I see the results. In fact, the gap between the haves and the
have-nots in the product launch world, in the online business world, has never been
bigger.
That’s what this video series is all about. I want you to show you what’s broken so you
can avoid that. It’s not hard to avoid these things. You’ll see in just a minute. I want to
show you what’s broken, but more important I want to show you what’s working so that
you can have a great launch.
Not just one great launch, but then you can stack the launches on top of each other.
You go from one to the next and you’re continually building more momentum and
continually building your results so that you can build a great business, so that you can
build a real business, a business that gives you the income, the lifestyle, and the impact
that you want and that you deserve.
The reality is that you are going to launch. You are going to launch. If you’re just
starting out, then obviously at some point you’re going to open your doors for business
and that’s your launch. If you’ve already got a business, you know that you need to
innovate; you need to innovate your offers and you need to innovate your products. You
are going to launch.

-- 3 of 12 --

The big question is are you going to use a well structured, well engineered launch, or
are you going to use what I call hope marketing? Hope is an interesting word. It could
save your life if you’re shipwrecked at sea. It’s really important that you have that hope
that you can hang onto something.
But in business, hope is an awful word. You never want to hope that your marketing
does okay. We see this hope marketing when someone puts all this time into creating a
product, they work on it and they work on it, and then they just sort of release it. They
just put it out to the world, it’s like Field of Dreams, build it and they will come. That’s
hope marketing and it doesn’t work. In almost every case you just sort of fade away
and die. That’s why you need to put the thought, you need a well structured, well
engineered launch.
There’s this other thing, the perfect product syndrome. Maybe you know what I’m
talking about. This is where you have that product that’s almost perfect, that’s 95%
done or 99% done, but you keep on polishing it up and changing this, changing that,
moving this around, trying to get it absolutely perfect. You have to avoid that perfect
product syndrome, because it will absolutely kill you. It’s the death of an entrepreneur.
It’s this albatross that’s hanging around your neck. You have to avoid that.
Instead of obsessing about having the perfect product, you need to obsess about
having the perfect client experience. Worry about their experience more than the
product. They can’t have that experience unless you release the product.
The perfect antidote to the perfect product syndrome is a great launch. In an upcoming
video I’m going to share with you all the different types of launches. You run people
through a launch and they get really super excited, you get so much feedback, you
start what we call this launch conversation. Then you use that to get your product out
there and get feedback on your product, and then you move on to version 2.0. The best
way to beat that perfect product syndrome is to just put it out there with a launch.
This is the question. Are you going to use hope marketing and start off slow and fade
away from there and wither away and just have your business die on you? Or are you
going to use a well structured, engineered product launch that builds momentum for
you, that gives you a surge of sales and that gives you great expert positioning in your
market?
The late, great Gary Halbert once said that you’re just one great sales letter away from
solving almost any business problem. That’s something I’ve always subscribed to, this
idea of where if I’ve got anything going on in my business, any problems, any
challenges, I just want to sell a whole bunch more stuff. The thing is Gary Halbert was
absolutely 100% correct when he said it. But times change and now you really need to
go sideways…
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 4 of 12 --

I’m sure you’ve seen some of those old style sales letter and it’s basically a big long
webpage. Before the web they were hard copy, but now you see this big long sales
letter that can be 10, 20, 30, 40 pages long. There’s a headline up here and there’s a
standard formula, a lot of times it’s what we call this PAS, which stands for Problem,
Agitate, Solve or Problem, Agitate, Solution.
That’s where you’re basically grabbing people’s attention with a headline, then you’re
defining some problem they have in their life. They bought a guitar, but they don’t
know how to play songs, or they don’t have a girlfriend, or their dog won’t come when
they call them. You define their problem, then you agitate them about the problem, you
make them feel a little worse about that problem. Then you give them the solution, but
you don’t really give them the solution, you sell them the solution.
This is that old style sales letter, and for years it worked really well. The thing is
nowadays what people do is they just grab the scroll bar and they just go down here
and look at what the price is and they decide if they want to buy it. These old style
sales letters are just not very effective and their effectiveness keeps on dropping off.
Let’s just say that’s a 10 page sales letter.
Now I want to show you the sideways sales letter. This is where we go sideways, this is
the big innovation that I brought to the market that literally changed marketing, it
changed the way we sold stuff. Instead of thinking about a 10 page sales letter that’s
long, think about it as a 10 day process, a 10 day launch.
Let’s pretend that I drew that right and there are 10 lines there. What we would do is
release three pieces of content, what we call PLC – that’s prelaunch content – PLC1,
PLC2, and PLC3.
So we’ve got a video here. They don’t have to be videos, these days we tend to use
videos because they’re just so effective, but these could actually be blog posts, they
could be emails. In the next video I’m going to be teaching you some of the different
styles of launches that we have, including the seed launch. Seed launch is awesome
because it’s how you can launch when you have no list and no product – it’s pretty cool.
Often in the seed launch we’re not using videos, we might just be using email.
Let’s just go standard style with three videos. You release that first video and then
maybe three days later you release the second video, three days later you release that
third video, and you’re doing something similar to what you would do with the sales
letter.
Remember I told you there were these broken launches. In these broken launches what
people do is they go literal with that sales letter, they take the sales letter and they split
it into three pieces and they’re just giving this long 10 days sales letter. The problem is
they don’t create any value.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 5
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 5 of 12 --

What you want to do is be creating real value as you move through these videos.
This first video is often all about the journey. It’s where you show people the
opportunity they can have to change their life – that they can learn to play the guitar,
that they can learn to play songs. You show them that opportunity for transformation
and then you show them what that journey will be like. All along the way you’re
teaching, you’re giving real value.
Then in the second video it’s the transformation video. That’s where you make the
transformation real. Oftentimes you’ll have case studies in here and you’ll show them
how they can really have that transformation.
In the third video it’s what we call the ownership experience. This is where you start to
pivot towards making that sale. You start to make that pivot and you show them what
it’s actually like to own your product and really have that change.
Remember problem, agitate, solve? They’ll do this problem, agitate, and then solve
here, what comes at the end is your sales video or sales letter, and you have that path
at the end. So it’s three pieces of prelaunch content leading into that. Well, the broken
launches will just define your problem for you, they’ll get you all agitated about it, and
then for the solution it’s “buy my stuff,” and it’s back to being like that old style sales
letter. When people come into your old style sales letter it’s like, “buy my stuff, buy my
stuff, buy my stuff.” That doesn’t work these days. What works these days is a
conversation. I’ll get to that in just a minute.
Instead of doing this onetime problem, agitate, solve, what we’ll do in a launch is we’ll
define a problem in this first piece of prelaunch content. We’ll define the problem and
then we’ll actually give the solution in that first of prelaunch content. And then towards
the end of it, we’ll define another problem.
Then in that second piece we’ll solve that problem. What we’re doing here is we’re
building up trust, we’re building up relationship, we’re building up rapport. At the end of
that second video we could define another problem, and we actually go ahead and
solve that problem again. Then at the end we define another problem. At that point,
the solution is to buy my stuff.
We’ve gone through this cycle and we’ve actually defined either two or three different
problems and through our prelaunch we’ve actually solved those problems. We’re giving
real content, we’re creating real value.
Those launches where it’s just one big “here’s your problem,” and get them all worked
up and then give the solution and the solution is “buy my stuff,” that creates this
complete value vacuum, there is no value whatsoever. That’s how you break a launch.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 6
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 6 of 12 --

Since there are so many launches, since it’s so effective, since so many people use this,
a lot of times people are just like, “I know, I’m going to do a launch. I’m just going to
create three videos.” And they create three videos that create no value and they just
think that they’re magically going to do a launch and it’s going to be great. That doesn’t
work.
Another thing that doesn’t work is just throwing up three random videos. “I have this
video I shot two years ago, and I have this video I shot last week, and I could throw
together one more video, and I’ll just throw out some videos and then a sales video and
it wins.” It doesn’t. You have to create this journey throughout this whole path where
you’re showing the transformation, where you’re continually building value, where
you’re defining a problem and you’re solving it. Define a problem, then solve it, and
move through it this way.
There’s one more thing I want to layer in here that really makes this whole strategy
pop, and that’s the mental triggers.
This is the deal. There are certain triggers that create enormous influence in us, in our
brains, that cause us to make decisions, that really cause us to take actions. These
mental triggers exist in every one of us and they have been with us as humans for
thousands of years, and it’s very likely they’ll be with us for thousands of years more.
No matter who you are, where you live in the world, these mental triggers definitely
have an impact on you and your decision making – and me and my decision making.
We’re all susceptible to these triggers.
Let me just go through a few of them. I actually have 20 of these that I’ve identified,
but I don’t have time to go through all 20 in this video. Let’s just go through a few of
the top ones.
First of all is community. Community is a very powerful trigger. It’s like if we perceive
ourselves as being part of a community we will act in accordance to how we think the
people in that community are supposed to act. Really powerful
Another one is anticipation. This is the idea where we anticipate something happening,
anticipating events. If you think about how powerful events are, like birthdays,
graduations, holidays, and how we look forward to those. Think about the way TV is,
almost any TV shows they have these cliffhangers at the end of the show that leave you
anticipating and pull you in for the next show. Even the most basic sitcoms use
anticipation.
Then there’s authority. Authority is an incredibly powerful trigger and it’s a great one to
use in your launches. The launch process really brings out the authority. It’s one of the
most powerful. That’s basically when we perceive someone as being an authority, we
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 7
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 7 of 12 --

listen to them. They are very influential. Almost any type of uniform, like a police officer
or a doctor, that creates authority and it’s very powerful.
Reciprocity is another great trigger. That’s the idea of it we give something to someone,
they’re going to feel an obligation to give something back to us. And it works the other
way around. If you give a gift to someone, they’re going to feel like they want to give a
gift back to you. If someone gives you a gift, you’re going to feel like you need to give
them a gift back.
Then there’s social proof. This is the idea where if we see a lot of other people doing
something then we’re going to think, “Maybe there’s something there,” and we’re going
to be more inclined to do that as well.
Finally, there’s scarcity. If there is less of something, then we want it more. Gold,
diamonds, Ferraris, whatever, just the fact that there’s fewer of something instantly
raises that value and gets us more interested.
Those triggers are everywhere in our lives. They work everywhere in our lives. But I
want to talk about how you can take these and layer them into your launch.
Here we have our sideways sales letter, we have our P.S. path there where we’re
providing great value, we’re defining their problems, giving them solutions to their
problems, building this relationship. One of the things is right off the bat when we give
some great value – and what I want you to do in your launch is give great value, give
some of your best content away in that very first piece – you instantly create this
authority.
They’re like, “Holy cow.” First of all, you’re just demonstrating how much you know,
how comfortable you are, and that just instantly builds authority. There’s this idea that
you can just give this stuff away for free, that’s massive authority.
Then right off the bat what you’re always doing is underneath each one of these you’re
building it in a way to get feedback. If you’re putting this video out, you have
comments down below the video. When there are comments down there and people
can see other people are watching your video and leaving comments, that builds up
social proof. That’s the idea of, “All these other people are watching this, there must be
something there and there must be something going on.”
Then as you move through here, you actually start to build up community. The
community actually develops down in those comments. You have comments down
below each one of these videos, people will start answering it, you can start answering
them. It also brings in another trigger that I didn’t mention, which is conversation.
People love a conversation. They would much rather be in a conversation than be
listening to a monologue.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 8
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 8 of 12 --

Of course, you’re also building reciprocity because you’re giving great content. As you
give great content, people are loving you and they want to give something back to you.
Then as you start to move through here and get deeper, you just keep on building
social proof, you keep on building community, but now you start to build anticipation.
People are looking forward to your final offer. You’re taking your marketing and you’re
turning into an event.
All through here you’re building this authority, now you’re starting to build anticipation.
Then also you want to build in some scarcity. As you get closer to the end, you
definitely want a start and a finish to your launch.
This is another place that people mess up their launches. When you have your launch it
has to be a very definitive start and an end to that launch. There has to be some
special deal here when you do open up your cart and start selling. There has to be an
end to that launch. They have to get some special deal and then that special deal ends.
Literally, that will double the sales in your launch if you have that hard deadline at the
end of the launch. That’s where scarcity starts to play a piece.
These mental triggers can be really powerful. You’re building authority all the way
through. You’re building social proof, because people can see all the interaction going
on and all the comments, they can feel that buzz, they can feel that community. What is
a community? It’s all this discussion that’s happening down below your videos.
Towards the end you build the anticipation, that social proof, that scarcity. When you
layer all of these on top of creating this path of problem-solution-problem-solution and
adding value, it’s just a really powerful model.
The reality is it’s a lot easier to sell to someone if they know, like, and trust you. I like
to think of it in terms of this B.S. detector. It’s like you walk onto a car lot, you see the
door open up and the salesperson is coming towards you and instantly your B.S.
detector is in the red zone. You’re not going to trust them – or maybe you trust them,
but you’re not going to trust them as well as you trust your best friend. On the other
hand, if your best friend says, “Hey, I just bought this new car. I love this new car. You
have to check it out, it’s completely awesome.” Your B.S. detector is backed way off,
you trust them, you believe them. Maybe you know they tend to have a different
opinion on things, but you definitely trust them.
The thing is that old style marketing, that old style sales letter, or even those broken
launches where you don’t provide any value, that B.S. detector is in the red zone. But if
you do this, if you show people their problem-solution-problem-solution, if you layer in
all these mental triggers, that B.S. detector backs way off into the safe zone.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 9
PLF_Module5v1.0

-- 9 of 12 --

Then when you actually do get to your sales letter or sales video it might spike up a
little bit, because you’re eventually going to ask for some money, you have to do that to
make the sale, but the reality is that B.S. detector stays a lot further down in the safe
zone because you’ve built up that relationship. What we’re really talking about is
creating a conversation with your market, delivering real value, creating a real
relationship and then asking for the sale.
That is the single most effective way for making sales, creating an impact, and building
a business that there is. The big question is what does it do for you? How are you going
to use this?
If you’re watching this, you probably fall into one of three categories.
First, you might have an online business, you’re already selling stuff, you’ve already got
some traffic to your website. If that’s the case then this is an awesome day for you,
because there’s just no other way to get more leverage quicker in your business.
Basically you take your existing offer, you can tweak it, you can add some bonuses,
maybe change the pricing up a little bit, and do a launch. Basically re-launch your
product, re-launch your business. Like I said, this is the absolute fastest way to increase
your leverage and just get big time results in no time whatsoever.
The second category is maybe you have some type of business where you might be a
practitioner, you might have a service based business where you’re working with clients,
but you don’t have leverage in your business, you don’t have products, you’re more
working with people. In that case, we’ve seen this over and over. You might be a
practitioner who is helping people with their wellness, their health, or their money, or
their business, or real estate, or their hobbies or their sports. In this case, what you
want to do is create some leverage. You want to create some products.
Just think about if you had a product that sold for $97 or $297 or $997, and you put
together a launch and you sold a whole bunch of those. How would that change your
business? How would that change your life? Then if you kept on making sales months
after month. That’s what this is all about.
If you think about it, you already have these clients. A bunch of them would probably
love to get some more of you, to buy some more of you, to give you some more money
for a product. Product Launch Formula can work fantastic for you. The sideways sales
letter is awesome, because you have these clients where you already have this
relationship.
The third category is maybe this all sounds great, “Jeff, I love it. It sounds amazing,”
but you don’t have a business and you don’t have a list, you don’t have a product, you
don’t have any kind of following, and maybe you don’t even have an idea. If this is you,
I’ve got a real soft spot for you because that’s where I was not very long ago. I was

actually Mr. Mom, I was at home taking care of a couple babies while my wife was out
making a living and supporting the family. I hadn’t made any kind of income in years.
Let me tell you, it is absolutely possible to build a real serious business.
If you look at what I’ve built where I have thousands of clients and I’ve made this
impact where my clients have done hundreds of millions of dollars of launches, it just
shows where it can go if you’re just starting like I was. If you’re just starting out, this
can absolutely work for you. In fact, the thing you’re going to want to do is what I call
the seed launch. I’m actually going to be teaching about that in my next video.
In my next video we’re going to go deep into the seed launch. That’s where you can
start with no list and no product, and you basically get paid to create that first product
and create that first list. How awesome is that?
I’ve used the seed launch over and over. In fact, I still use it. I’ve used it recently. The
seed launch, if you already have a business, it’s great if you want to test out a new
product idea or if you want to get paid to create the product. The seed launch isn’t just
for starting out, but it’s great for starting out. I’m going to be going deep into the seed
launch in my next video.
I’m also going to cover launch selection. I showed you the sideways sales letter, but
how does that actually fit right into launch. The thing is there’s a number of different
launches; I have six primary types of launches. In the next video I’m going to be going
through launch selection and showing you where each type of launch can fit in, because
you have to pick the right launch, you can’t just throw a bunch of videos out there.
We’ve already seen that. You can’t just do any kind of launch, there are specific
launches based on your specific situation. I’m going to be going into that in the next
video.
Then, super important, is the launch stacking. I’m going to cover launch stacking in the
next video. That’s this idea if you do this first launch and then the next launch stacks on
top of it and generally your results get better as you move along. Your results get
better, you get more influence, you make a bigger impact, your business grows as you
go from launch to launch. That’s how I went from my very first launch, which did
$1,650 – and when I did that I was really happy about, I was doing backflips over
$1,650. I went from that first launch and built on it and built on it, and I eventually did
a launch that did millions of dollars. That’s all about launch stacking and that’s in the
next video.
First, right now what I would love you to do is scroll down, hit that Facebook like, hit
that share button, because everyone loves to get likes. And then what I really want you
to do is scroll down and leave a comment.
